entity: Investigative Case Management (ICM), FALCON, Immigration OS, & Enhanced Leads Identification and Targeting for Enforcement (ELITE)
description: This suite of Palantir-designed data analysis tools ingests massive troves of data, tracks agents’ investigative activities, sorts tips, assesses where immigrants likely live, tracks visa overstays, and allows agents to quickly access data on millions of people. DHS uses them to determine whom to target with their operations and where.

description: The Repository for Analytics in a Virtualized Environment (RAVEn) allows users to use a single query to search multiple federal databases as well as commercial and open-source information. This combination of massive amounts of data allows agents to create detailed profiles of criminal suspects, immigrants, or anyone else.

description: This new tool is designed to provide DHS with real-time and historical location information and threat assessments of investigative targets, including those suspected only of illegal re-entry, although questions persist about the vendor’s past work and ability to actually produce such a product.

sourceLinks.label: The Lever: The ICE Surveillance Firm with Missing Executives and Phantom Clients (May 2026)
The Lever: Inside ICE’s $12 Million Plan to Map Immigrants’ “Patterns of Life” (Apr. 2026)

description: This system is designed to investigate people suspected of violating the length or other terms of their visas by organizing disparate information about the same person into one place.

sourceLinks.label: FedScoop: ICE Seeks Support for App that Tracks Visa, Immigration Violators (Feb. 2020)

description: This system combines prison release information, other government databases, and commercial data to locate individuals eligible for deportation.

sourceLinks.label: EPIC: DHS's Data Reservoir: ICE and CBP's Capture and Circulation of Location Information, page 24 (Aug. 2022)

description: DHS utilizes numerous other commercial tools, internal systems, and AI capabilities to analyze data in its possession. This includes capabilities to score the relative value of investigative information, clean databases to compile complete information on individuals, draw insights from communications records, and more.
